documentation.value:
Defines an environment variable.
```dotenv
DATABASE_URL=postgres://user:pass@localhost:5432/mydb
API_KEY=sk-abc123
DEBUG=true
```
Variable name. Convention: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE. Must not contain spaces or `=`.
The value. Can be unquoted, single-quoted (literal), or double-quoted (allows `\n` escapes and variable expansion).

Double-quoted values support escape sequences and variable interpolation.
```dotenv
GREETING="Hello World"
MULTILINE="line1\nline2"
FULL_URL="https://${HOST}:${PORT}/api"
```
Variable name.
Value in double quotes. Supports `\n`, `\r`, `\t` escapes and `${VAR}` interpolation.

Single-quoted values are treated as literals with no interpolation or escape processing.
```dotenv
REGEX='\d+\.\d+'
PATH_PATTERN='/home/*/data'
```
Variable name.
Literal value. No escape sequences or variable expansion.

Exports the variable to the shell environment (for compatibility with `source .env`).
```dotenv
export NODE_ENV=production
export PATH="/usr/local/bin:$PATH"
```
Variable name to export.
The value to assign.
Variable name.
The variable to reference.
Default value if VARIABLE is unset or empty.

Lines starting with `#` are comments and are ignored.
```dotenv
# Database configuration
DB_HOST=localhost
DB_PORT=5432  # inline comments are also supported in some implementations
```
Comment text. Used for documentation and section headers.

Multi-line values using double quotes and `\n` or heredoc-style.
```dotenv
PRIVATE_KEY="-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----\nMIIE...\n-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----"

# Or with actual newlines in double quotes (some parsers):
CERT="-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
MIID...
-----END CERTIFICATE-----"
```
Variable name.
Multi-line content using `\n` escapes within double quotes.

Sets a variable to an empty string.
```dotenv
OPTIONAL_FLAG=
FALLBACK_URL=
```
Variable name set to an empty string.
